content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	
5	prover (iteration 073 — four parallel lanes).
6	
7	## Stages
8	- [x] init
9	- [x] autoformalize
10	- [ ] prover
11	- [ ] polish
12	
13	## Big-picture context
14	
15	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
16	
17	`STRATEGY.md` describes the five-phase build-out plan. After iter-072's four-lane prover round (net 19 → 16 sorries, -3), the sorry landscape is:
18	
19	| File | Sorries | Phase | Status |
20	|---|---|---|---|
21	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` | **6** | A | iter-073 Lane 2 |
22	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` | **7** | B | iter-073 Lane 1 |
23	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` | **1** | C (deferred) / D refactor | iter-073 Lane 3 (refactor) |
24	| `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` | 1 | E (closes by reduction) | iter-073 Lane 4 |
25	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` | 1 | C-rep (deferred) | off-limits |
26	| **Total** | **16** | | |
27	
28	Iter-072 achievements:
29	- Lane 1 — `JacobianWitness` parameterised uniformly over the marked point `P` (renamed `isAlbanese → isAlbaneseFor : ∀ P, IsAlbanese …`, dropped internal `P` field). Sorry count 1 → 1 (planned).
30	- Lane 2 — `AbelJacobi.lean` 3 → 1; `ofCurve`/`comp_ofCurve` fully closed and `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` reduced to a single genus-0 rigidity sorry.
31	- Lane 3 — `Differentials.lean` 8 → 7; `cotangentExactSeqAlpha` inner sorry (L242) closed via the adjunction-coherence identity `φ_g' ≫ f.c = adj_f.unit ≫ (pushforward).map φ_fg'` (which holds `rfl`-style for this composition) and `isUniversal'.desc`.
32	- Lane 4 — `BasicOpenCech.lean` 6 → 6 (no net change but structurally improved); hoisted `Z_i`/`e_i`/`h_mod_pi_i` to outer scope, structurally closed `f_R`/`g_R map_smul'` via `Equiv.smul_def` + `e_i.injective` transport, leaving two named helper-claim sorries `h_diff_pi_smul_{f,g}` at L996/L1004 that capture only the per-restriction R-linearity content.
33	
34	## Current Objectives
35	
36	The dispatcher fans out one prover per file listed below. Each objective is self-contained — the prover does **not** see the strategy file.
37	
38	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` — Phase B middle (cotangent exactness)
39	
40	**File status.** 7 syntactic sorries, ~590 LOC. Theorems under attack: `cotangentExactSeq_structure` sub-claims `h_zero` (L395) and `h_exact` (L399), both freshly unblocked by iter-072's closure of `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`.
41	
42	**Iter-073 target.** Close `h_zero` and `h_exact` in `cotangentExactSeq_structure` (L380-L404). The lemma destructures into three `refine`-introduced sub-goals:
43	
44	```
45	refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
46	· -- h_zero : α ≫ β = 0       (L395 sorry)
47	· -- h_exact : ShortComplex(α, β).Exact  (L399 sorry)
48	· -- h_epi : Epi β             (L404 sorry — stretch this iteration)
49	```
50	
51	**Strategy per sub-claim.**
52	
53	- **`h_zero` (L395).** On each affine chart `A → A' → B`, the composition `α ≫ β` becomes `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange A A' B ≫ KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B`. The local statement `mapBaseChange ≫ map = 0` follows from `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` (the range of `mapBaseChange` is contained in the kernel of `map`). The global statement is then `α ≫ β = 0 : Sheaf.Hom` by presheaf-of-modules extensionality at each open. The substantive work is:
54	  1. Unfold `cotangentExactSeqAlpha` and `cotangentExactSeqBeta` to expose the underlying `presheafHom := isUniversal'.desc d_i` description.
55	  2. Reduce the composition statement at each open `U` to the ring-level `mapBaseChange ≫ map`.
56	  3. Apply `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` (Mathlib `Algebra/Kaehler/CotangentComplex.lean`) — this is the well-known relative cotangent exact sequence.
57	  
58	- **`h_exact` (L399).** Same ring-level input: `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` says the range of `mapBaseChange` *equals* the kernel of `map`. Globalises by the same presheaf-of-modules extensionality argument as `h_zero`. The structural pattern is: `ShortComplex.Exact` reduces to pointwise homology-zero, then to the ring-level exact sequence.
59	
60	- **Stretch: `h_epi` (L404).** Independent of `h_zero`/`h_exact`. The local epi statement is `KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B` surjective, which is `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective` (Mathlib `RingTheory/Kaehler/Basic.lean`). Globalising requires a `SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf` helper plus `PresheafOfModules.epi_iff_surjective`. Mathlib does *not* appear to have a direct `SheafOfModules.epi_iff_epi_presheaf` lemma; the prover may need to add a local lemma using the abstraction layer of `Sheafify`/conservativity. If this stretch is too involved, leave `h_epi` for a future iteration and report partial closure.
61	
62	**Mathlib leverage names** (verified iter-072):
63	- `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Kaehler/CotangentComplex.lean`) — relative cotangent exact sequence at the ring level.
64	- `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective` (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Kaehler/Basic.lean`).
65	- `PresheafOfModules.epi_iff_surjective` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/EpiMono.lean:59`).
66	- `PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal'`, `derivation'` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Differentials/Presheaf.lean:216`, `210`).
67	- `ShortComplex.Exact`, `ShortComplex.mk_exact_of_isZero_homology`.
68	
69	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex` (`lem:cotangent_exact_structure`).
70	
71	**Hard constraints.** Protected signatures frozen. No new axioms. Per user policy 2026-05-11, do not pre-run `lean_run_code` on candidate bodies, and do not extract chains of thin helpers across iterations.
72	
73	**Do NOT attempt this iteration**:
74	- `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` (L122) — downstream.
75	- `smooth_iff_locally_free_omega` (L449), `cotangent_at_section` (L466), `serre_duality_genus` (L608) — downstream of cotangent exact sequence + Phase A genus carrier.
76	
77	### 2. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — Phase A continuation
78	
79	**File status.** 6 syntactic sorries, ~1090 LOC. Theorems under attack: `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L996) and `h_diff_pi_smul_g` (L1004), the two named helper sub-claims introduced in iter-072 that isolate the per-restriction R-linearity content of the alternating-coface Čech differential.
80	
81	**Iter-073 target.** Close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` and `h_diff_pi_smul_g`. Each is of the shape:
82	```
83	∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z_in i),
84	  e_out (⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.{f,g}) (e_in.symm (r • y))) =
85	    r • e_out (⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.{f,g}) (e_in.symm y))
86	```
87	asserting that the Čech differential `scK₀.{f,g}`, viewed in the explicit product representation via `e_in`/`e_out`, is R-linear on each component.
88	
89	**Strategy.**
90	
91	1. **Unfold the differential.** `scK₀.{f,g}` is the Čech differential at a specific degree of the alternating-coface complex on the `↑s₀`-indexed cover. Unfold the chain `cechCochain → cechComplexFunctor.obj → alternatingCofaceMapComplex.comp cosimplicialObjectFunctor` to expose the `d`-map as an alternating sum:
92	   ```
93	   d_n = ∑_{k=0}^{n+1} (-1)^k · δ_k
94	   ```
95	   where each `δ_k` at degree `n` is a `Pi.lift` of presheaf restrictions across the cover's intersection pattern.
96	
97	2. **Per-summand R-linearity.** Each `δ_k`, viewed through `e_in`/`e_out`, becomes a `Pi.map (restriction)` term composed with a permutation/projection. Each restriction `(C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE _).op).hom : Γ(C.left, V) → Γ(C.left, W)` is an R-algebra-hom via the project-local `Scheme.algebraSection` / `algebraMap_naturality` chain (see `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` L161 for `algebraMap_naturality`), hence R-linear.
98	
99	3. **Closure mechanics.** `LinearMap.pi` of R-linear maps is R-linear pointwise. Sums (including alternating sums) of R-linear maps are R-linear. Combine via `LinearMap.add_apply`, `LinearMap.smul_apply`, and `Finset.sum_smul_comm`.
100	
101	**Mathlib + project leverage names** (verified iter-068+ analogy):
102	- `alternatingCofaceMapComplex.d`, `cosimplicialObjectFunctor` (`Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/AlternatingFaceMapComplex.lean`).
103	- `LinearMap.pi`, `LinearMap.proj`, `LinearMap.coe_pi`, `LinearMap.pi_apply`.
104	- `Scheme.algebraSection`, `algebraMap_naturality` (`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` L80/L101 — project-local).
105	- `ModuleCat.piIsoPi`, `LinearEquiv.toAddEquiv`, `AddEquiv.module`.
106	
107	**Stretch (if helpers land quickly).** With `h_diff_pi_smul_{f,g}` closed, `h_loc_exact` (L1083) becomes the last remaining Cluster (b)+(c) sorry. Strategy: `IsLocalizedModule.pi` over the finite indexing set `Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀`, with the per-factor localisation provided by `localizedModuleIsLocalizedModule` (the same lemma used for `h_loc_X_i`). Then `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` transports `h_a₀_fun f` from the slice cover to the localised cochain. Estimate: ~50-100 LOC. Mark as stretch — do not block on it.
108	
109	**Untouched sorries this iteration** (confirmed dead-ends across multiple iterations):
110	- L495 (substep (a), `s`-indexed cover, extra-degeneracy).
111	- L819 (`h_π_split` analogue / refinement transport `s → s₀`).
112	- L847 (substep (a), `↑s₀`-indexed cover, extra-degeneracy).
113	
114	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex` § *Čech acyclicity for the structure sheaf on affine basic-open covers*. Mathematical content + Mathlib leverage names also in `analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`.
115	
116	**Hard constraints.** Protected signatures (`basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`) frozen. No new axioms. Transient-sorry policy in force. Per user policy 2026-05-11, do not pre-run `lean_run_code` on candidate bodies and do not chain thin helpers across iterations (the helper-claim isolation in iter-072 was a one-time decomposition, not a chain).
117	
118	### 3. `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` — Phase D refactor (precursor to Lane 4)
119	
120	**File status.** 1 syntactic sorry, ~238 LOC. Theorem under attack: structural refactor of `Jacobian` and its four protected instances (no sorry closure; the single sorry `nonempty_jacobianWitness` at L172 stays).
121	
122	**Iter-073 target.** **Remove the `if h : genus C = 0 then 𝟙_ _ else (jacobianWitness C).J` dite from the `Jacobian` definition.** Refactor as follows:
123	
124	1. **`Jacobian` definition** (current L189-L198):
125	   ```
126	   noncomputable def Jacobian (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
127	       [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
128	     if h : genus C = 0 then
129	       𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))
130	     else
131	       (jacobianWitness C).J
132	   ```
133	   becomes:
134	   ```
135	   noncomputable def Jacobian (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
136	       [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
137	     (jacobianWitness C).J
138	   ```
139	
140	2. **Four protected instances** (`instGrpObj` L206, `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus` L214, `instIsProper` L222, `instGeometricallyIrreducible` L229) currently use `unfold Jacobian; split_ifs`. Refactor each to project unconditionally from the witness:
141	   ```
142	   instance instGrpObj : GrpObj (Jacobian C) := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
143	   instance smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (Jacobian C).hom :=
144	     (jacobianWitness C).smoothGenus
145	   instance instIsProper : IsProper (Jacobian C).hom := (jacobianWitness C).proper
146	   instance instGeometricallyIrreducible : GeometricallyIrreducible (Jacobian C).hom :=
147	     (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
148	   ```
149	   (Modulo `Jacobian C = (jacobianWitness C).J` reducing definitionally, which it does after the dite removal.)
150

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Search Protocol
66	
67	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
68	
69	1. `lean_local_search` first
70	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
71	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
72	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
73	
74	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
75	
76	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
77	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
78	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
79	
80	## Logging
81	
82	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
83	
84	**Format:**
85	
86	```markdown
87	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
88	
89	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
90	### Attempt 1
91	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
92	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
93	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
94	
95	### Attempt 2
96	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
97	- **Result:** RESOLVED
98	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
99	
100	## helper_bijective (line 78)
101	### Attempt 1
102	- **Approach:** Function.Bijective.comp
103	- **Result:** IN PROGRESS — stuck on surjectivity
104	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective
105	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
106	```
107	
108	**Rules:**
109	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
110	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
111	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
112	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
113	
114	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
115	
116	## Summary Pipeline
117	
118	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
119	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
120	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
121	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
122	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
123	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
124	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
125	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
130	
131	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
132	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
133	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
134	   - Concrete next step for the next session
135	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
136	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)

6	import Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic
7	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme
8	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.FinitePresentation
9	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth
10	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Presheaf
11	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf
12	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Presheaf
13	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf
14	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK
15	
16	/-! # Relative Kähler differentials for schemes
17	
18	This file constructs the sheaf of relative Kähler differentials `Ω_{X/S}`
19	of a morphism of schemes `f : X → S`, together with its universal derivation
20	`d : 𝒪_X → Ω_{X/S}`. It also states the cotangent exact sequence and the
21	characterisation of smoothness in terms of local freeness of `Ω`.
22	
23	The construction builds on Mathlib's ring-theoretic `KaehlerDifferential`
24	and the presheaf-of-modules differential construction
25	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`.
26	
27	## Status (iteration 064 — scaffold)
28	
29	All main declarations have `sorry` bodies. Closure trajectory is estimated
30	at ~10 iterations per `STRATEGY.md`.
31	
32	## References
33	
34	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
35	-/
36	
37	set_option autoImplicit false
38	
39	universe u
40	
41	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
42	
43	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
44	
45	variable {X Y S : Scheme.{u}}
46	
47	/-! ## The relative cotangent presheaf and sheaf -/
48	
49	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
50	
51	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
52	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
53	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
54	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
55	
56	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
57	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
58	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
59	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
60	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
61	    f.c
62	  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'
63	
64	/-! ### Sheaf condition: decomposition
65	
66	Closing `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` is the main outstanding
67	gap of Phase B step 1. The mathematical proof has three layers:
68	
69	1. **`KaehlerDifferential` commutes with localisation.** For a ring map
70	   `A → B` and `f ∈ B`, `Ω_{B[1/f]/A} ≅ Ω_{B/A} ⊗_B B[1/f]`. Mathlib's
71	   `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule` (and the scheme variant
72	   `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`) provides this.
73	2. **The presheaf is a sheaf on the basis of affine basic opens.** On a
74	   basic open `D(f)` of `Spec B`, sections are `Ω_{B[1/f]/A}`. Gluing on a
75	   basic-open cover `{D(fᵢ)}` of `Spec B` follows from the localisation
76	   compatibility of step 1, applied to each `fᵢ`.
77	3. **Globalisation to all opens.** A presheaf on a scheme is a sheaf iff
78	   it is a sheaf on the basis of affine opens (more precisely, on the
79	   basic opens of every affine open). This is the standard scheme
80	   sheaf-of-modules construction.
81	
82	The substep theorems below are the natural decomposition. Each remains
83	`sorry` pending the full proof; the structure mirrors the blueprint at
84	`blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`. -/
85	
86	/-- Substep 1 (localisation compatibility): For an affine open `V` of `X`
87	mapped to an affine open `U` of `S`, the sections of
88	`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf` over `V` are isomorphic (as additive groups)
89	to the Kähler differential module `KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app (op V))`. -/
90	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler (f : X ⟶ S)
91	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
92	    ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj V : Type _) =
93	      CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential
94	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat
95	          f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c |>.app V) :=
96	  rfl
97	
98	/-- The presheaf of relative differentials is a **sheaf** in the Zariski
99	topology: Kähler differentials commute with localisation, which gives the
100	gluing axiom on affine opens.
101	
102	**Status (iter-065):** Sorry. Proof requires combining:
103	- Substep 1 (`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler`, definitional)
104	- Substep 2: sheaf condition on the basis of basic opens, derived from
105	  `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule`
106	- Substep 3: globalisation from a basis to all opens (Mathlib has
107	  `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.isSheafUniqueGluing` and `isSheafFor` lemmas
108	  for restricting to a basis)
109	
110	The route via `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` is not viable since that
111	requires the result we are trying to prove (the presheaf must already be
112	a sheaf to be packaged as a `SheafOfModules`). -/
113	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf (f : X ⟶ S) :
114	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat)
115	      (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf := by
116	  -- Strategy: reduce to the underlying presheaf of types via
117	  -- `isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp`, then verify the sheaf condition on
118	  -- affine opens using `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`.
119	  -- The proof requires substantial development of a
120	  -- "sheaf condition restricted to affine basis" lemma + the localisation
121	  -- compatibility for Kähler differentials in the scheme-language.
122	  sorry
123	
124	/-- The sheaf of relative differentials `Ω_{X/S}`.
125	
126	Packages the presheaf `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf` with the sheaf axiom
127	`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` into the standard `X.Modules` shape. -/
128	noncomputable def relativeDifferentials (f : X ⟶ S) : X.Modules :=
129	  ⟨relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f, relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf f⟩
130	
131	/-! ## Universal derivation -/
132	
133	/-- The universal derivation `d : 𝒪_X → Ω_{X/S}`.
134	
135	On each affine open `V = Spec B` over `Spec A`, this is the Mathlib universal
136	derivation `d_{B/A} : B → Ω_{B/A}`. It is an `A`-linear sheaf map satisfying
137	the Leibniz rule `d(ab) = a d(b) + b d(a)`. -/
138	noncomputable def universalDerivation (f : X ⟶ S) :
139	    X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat ⟶
140	      (relativeDifferentials f).val.presheaf := by
141	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
142	    f.c
143	  let d' := PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ'
144	  have h_eq : X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := rfl
145	  refine {
146	    app := fun U ↦ AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (d'.d (X := U)),
147	    naturality := fun U V g ↦ ?naturality
148	  }
149	  case naturality =>
150	    ext x
151	    simp only [sheafCompose_obj_obj, PresheafOfModules.presheaf_obj_coe, Functor.comp_obj,
152	      CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj, Functor.comp_map, AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp]
153	    suffices d'.d ((ConcreteCategory.hom (X.presheaf.map g)) x) =
154	        (ConcreteCategory.hom ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).map g)) (d'.d x) by
155	      simpa using this
156	    exact d'.d_map g x
157	
158	/-! ## Cotangent exact sequence
159	
160	The cotangent exact sequence
161	```
162	  f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y} ⟶ 0
163	```
164	for a composition `X ⟶ Y ⟶ S` is decomposed into five named sub-claims:
165	
166	1. `cotangentExactSeqAlpha` — the base-change cotangent map
167	   `f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S}`. Locally, this is
168	   `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange A A' B`, where `A → A' → B` is the
169	   tower of structure ring maps on an affine chart.
170	2. `cotangentExactSeqBeta` — the relative-quotient cotangent map
171	   `Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y}`. Locally, this is the surjection
172	   `KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B` induced by the inclusion `A → A'`
173	   that kills the `A`-linear derivations factoring through `A'`.
174	3. `cotangentExactSeqAlpha_comp_Beta` — composition zero `α ≫ β = 0`,
175	   from the ring-level `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` (the
176	   range of `mapBaseChange` lies in the kernel of `map`).
177	4. `cotangentExactSeqShortComplexExact` — exactness `ker β = im α`,
178	   from the same ring-level theorem and gluing.
179	5. `cotangentExactSeqBetaEpi` — `β` is an epi, from
180	   `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective` applied on each affine chart.
181	
182	The headline theorem `cotangent_exact_sequence` then assembles the
183	existential bundle from these five components without further sorries. -/
184	
185	/-- The base-change cotangent map `f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S}`.
186	
187	For a composition `X ⟶ Y ⟶ S`, this is the natural map whose local
188	description on an affine chart with rings `A = O_S(W)`, `A' = O_Y(f(V))`,
189	`B = O_X(V)` is `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange A A' B`:
190	```
191	  Ω_{A'/A} ⊗_{A'} B ⟶ Ω_{B/A}
192	```
193	sending `da ⊗ b ↦ b · da` (where the right-hand `da` is the universal
194	derivation in `Ω_{B/A}`).
195	
196	**Status:** sorry — the global construction requires gluing the local
197	base-change maps, mediated by `PresheafOfModules.homMk` applied to the
198	ring-level `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange`. -/
199	noncomputable def cotangentExactSeqAlpha (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
200	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (relativeDifferentials g) ⟶
201	      relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g) := by
202	  -- Strategy. Apply the `pullback ⊣ pushforward` adjunction to convert the goal into the
203	  -- adjoint hom on `Y`, then build that hom from its underlying presheaf-of-modules morphism via
204	  -- the universal property of `relativeDifferentials' φ_g'`.
205	  --
206	  -- After `homEquiv.symm`, the goal becomes
207	  --   `relativeDifferentials g ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj (relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g))`,
208	  -- both `Y.Modules`. Their underlying presheaves are
209	  --   LHS.val = relativeDifferentialsPresheaf g
210	  --           = PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ_g'
211	  --   RHS.val = (PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
212	  --               (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g))
213	  -- so it suffices to build a `PresheafOfModules` morphism between the underlying values, then
214	  -- package via `⟨·⟩` as a `SheafOfModules.Hom`.
215	  refine ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _).symm ?_
216	  -- Set up the two ring presheaf morphisms that drive the relativeDifferentials' construction.
217	  let φ_g' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat g.base).homEquiv
218	    S.presheaf Y.presheaf).symm g.c
219	  let φ_fg' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).homEquiv
220	    S.presheaf X.presheaf).symm (f ≫ g).c
221	  -- The presheaf-level morphism is the descent of the universal derivation of
222	  -- `relativeDifferentials' φ_g'` to the target, where the target carries the natural
223	  -- `Derivation' φ_g'` structure obtained by precomposing the universal derivation
224	  -- `D_{(f ≫ g)/S}` with the ring presheaf morphism `f.c.app U : Y.presheaf(U) → X.presheaf(f⁻¹U)`.
225	  --
226	  -- That is: define `d_target.d (U) (b) := D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom b)` where
227	  -- `D_X = PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_fg'`.
228	  -- The Leibniz (`d_mul`) and naturality (`d_map`) axioms follow respectively from the
229	  -- ring-hom property of `f.c.app U` and the naturality of `f.c`, together with the
230	  -- derivation laws on `D_X`.
231	  -- The vanishing condition `d (φ_g'.app U a) = 0` follows from the adjunction-coherence
232	  -- identity `(f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom.app U) ∘ (φ_g'.app U)
233	  --   = (φ_fg'.app (f⁻¹U)) ∘ (pullback-naturality map)`
234	  -- (an instance of the `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` naturality across the composition
235	  -- `f ≫ g`), combined with `D_X.d_app`.
236	  -- Substep (i): produce the morphism of presheaves of Y-modules.
237	  -- This is the substantive sorry; the decomposition above splits naturally into four named
238	  -- subgoals (`d`, `d_mul`, `d_map`, `d_app`) once the `Derivation'` constructor is unfolded.
239	  let D_X := PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_fg'
240	  let adj_f := TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base
241	  let d_target :
242	      ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
243	        (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g))).Derivation' φ_g' :=
244	    { d := fun {U} => AddMonoidHom.mk' (fun b => D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom b)) (by intro a b; simp)
245	      d_mul := by
246	        intro U a b
247	        dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
248	        rw [(f.c.app U).hom.map_mul, D_X.d_mul]
249	        rfl
250	      d_map := by
251	        intro U V f' x
252	        dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
253	        have hnat' : (f.c.app V).hom (Y.presheaf.map f' x) =
254	            X.presheaf.map ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).map f'.unop).op
255	              ((f.c.app U).hom x) := by
256	          have := congr_arg (fun h => ConcreteCategory.hom h x) (f.c.naturality f')
257	          simpa using this
258	        rw [hnat']
259	        exact (D_X.d_map _ _).symm
260	      d_app := by
261	        intro U a
262	        dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
263	        -- Adjunction-coherence: `φ_g' ≫ f.c = adj_f.unit ≫ (pushforward f).map φ_fg'` (rfl).
264	        have hcomm : φ_g' ≫ f.c =
265	            (adj_f.unit.app ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat g.base).obj S.presheaf)) ≫
266	              (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ_fg' := rfl
267	        have hcomm_app : (f.c.app U).hom (φ_g'.app U a) =
268	            (φ_fg'.app (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))).hom
269	              ((adj_f.unit.app ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat g.base).obj S.presheaf)).app
270	                U a) := by
271	          have h1 := congr_arg (fun h => ConcreteCategory.hom (NatTrans.app h U) a) hcomm
272	          simpa using h1
273	        rw [hcomm_app]
274	        exact D_X.d_app _ }
275	  let presheafHom : relativeDifferentialsPresheaf g ⟶
276	      (PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
277	        (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g)) :=
278	    (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').desc d_target
279	  exact ⟨presheafHom⟩

281	/-- The relative-quotient cotangent map `Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y}`.
282	
283	For a composition `X ⟶ Y ⟶ S`, this is the natural surjection whose
284	local description on an affine chart with rings `A = O_S(W)`,
285	`A' = O_Y(f(V))`, `B = O_X(V)` is `KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B`:
286	```
287	  Ω_{B/A} ⟶ Ω_{B/A'}
288	```
289	sending the universal `A`-linear derivation to the universal `A'`-linear
290	derivation.
291	
292	**Status:** sorry — the global construction follows from the morphism of
293	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
294	induced by the natural transformation `(f ≫ g)⁻¹ O_S ⟶ f⁻¹ O_Y` between
295	the pullback ring presheaves. -/
296	noncomputable def cotangentExactSeqBeta (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
297	    relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g) ⟶ relativeDifferentials f := by
298	  let φ1' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).homEquiv
299	    S.presheaf X.presheaf).symm (f ≫ g).c
300	  let φ2' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv
301	    Y.presheaf X.presheaf).symm f.c
302	  let adj_f := TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base
303	  let adj_fg := TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base
304	  let η := ((adj_fg.homEquiv S.presheaf ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj
305	    Y.presheaf)).symm (g.c ≫ (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map
306	      (adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf)))
307	  have hη : η ≫ φ2' = φ1' := by
308	    have h1 : adj_fg.homEquiv S.presheaf X.presheaf (η ≫ φ2') =
309	        adj_fg.homEquiv S.presheaf ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj Y.presheaf) η ≫
310	          (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).map φ2' := by
311	      rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right]
312	    have h2 : adj_fg.homEquiv S.presheaf ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj Y.presheaf) η =
313	        g.c ≫ (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map (adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf) := by
314	      dsimp [η]
315	      exact Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _
316	    have h3 : (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).map φ2' =
317	        (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map
318	          ((TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ2') := rfl
319	    have h4 : adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫ (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ2' = f.c := by
320	      have : adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫ (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ2' =
321	          adj_f.homEquiv Y.presheaf X.presheaf φ2' := rfl
322	      rw [this]
323	      exact Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _
324	    have h5 : adj_fg.homEquiv S.presheaf X.presheaf (η ≫ φ2') = (f ≫ g).c := by
325	      rw [h1, h2, h3]
326	      have h6 : (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map (adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf) ≫
327	          (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map
328	            ((TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ2') =
329	          (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map f.c := by
330	        rw [← Functor.map_comp]
331	        congr 1
332	        exact h4
333	      calc
334	        (g.c ≫ (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map (adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf)) ≫
335	            (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map
336	              ((TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ2') =
337	          g.c ≫ ((TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map (adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf) ≫
338	            (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map
339	              ((TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ2')) := by
340	          simp only [Category.assoc]
341	        _ = g.c ≫ (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map f.c := by erw [h6]
342	        _ = (f ≫ g).c := by exact (LocallyRingedSpace.comp_c f g).symm
343	    have h7 : adj_fg.homEquiv S.presheaf X.presheaf φ1' = (f ≫ g).c := by
344	      dsimp [φ1']
345	      exact Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _
346	    rw [← h7] at h5
347	    exact (adj_fg.homEquiv S.presheaf X.presheaf).injective h5
348	  let d2 := PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ2'
349	  let d1 : (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ2').Derivation' φ1' := {
350	    d := d2.d
351	    d_mul := d2.d_mul
352	    d_map := d2.d_map
353	    d_app := fun {X} a => by
354	      have h : φ1'.app X a = φ2'.app X (η.app X a) := by
355	        rw [← hη]
356	        rfl
357	      rw [h]
358	      exact d2.d_app (η.app X a)
359	  }
360	  let presheafHom := (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ1').desc d1
361	  exact ⟨presheafHom⟩

363	/-- The composition `α ≫ β = 0` of the two cotangent maps.
364	
365	On each affine chart `A → A' → B`, this is the statement that
366	`KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange A A' B ≫ KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B = 0`,
367	which follows directly from `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`
368	(the range of the first map is contained in the kernel of the second).
369	
370	**Status:** sorry — bundles the three remaining claims (composition zero,
371	exactness of the short complex, and `β` being an epi). All three are
372	local properties whose ring-level inputs are:
373	- composition zero: `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` (the
374	  range of `mapBaseChange` lies in the kernel of `map`)
375	- exactness: same theorem (the range equals the kernel)
376	- epi from `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective R S B`
377	
378	A future iteration may split this bundled theorem into three named
379	sub-claims once the ingredients land. -/
380	lemma cotangentExactSeq_structure (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
381	    ∃ (h : cotangentExactSeqAlpha f g ≫ cotangentExactSeqBeta f g = 0),
382	      (CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.mk
383	        (cotangentExactSeqAlpha f g) (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g) h).Exact ∧
384	      CategoryTheory.Epi (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g) := by
385	  -- Decomposition: produce the three named sub-claims in order. Each has a distinct
386	  -- ring-theoretic input via `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` / `map_surjective`.
387	  -- The composition-zero witness depends on the concrete definition of `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`,
388	  -- so closure here is blocked behind that definition's completion.
389	  refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
390	  · -- Sub-claim `h_zero` : `α ≫ β = 0`.
391	    -- On each affine chart `A → A' → B`, this is the local statement
392	    -- `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange A A' B ≫ KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B = 0`,
393	    -- which follows from `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` (range of `mapBaseChange`
394	    -- lies in `ker map`). Globalises via presheaf morphism extensionality.
395	    sorry
396	  · -- Sub-claim `h_exact` : the short complex is exact at the middle term.
397	    -- Same ring-level input (`KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`: range equals kernel),
398	    -- glued pointwise.
399	    sorry
400	  · -- Sub-claim `h_epi` : `β` is an epimorphism.
401	    -- Local statement: `KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B` is surjective (Mathlib's
402	    -- `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective`). For sheaves of modules, an epimorphism can be
403	    -- verified locally on a basis of opens (mirror of `Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`).
404	    sorry

406	/-- Cotangent exact sequence for a composition of schemes `X ⟶ Y ⟶ S`.
407	
408	For `f : X ⟶ Y` and `g : Y ⟶ S`, there is an exact sequence of
409	quasi-coherent `𝒪_X`-modules
410	```
411	  f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y} ⟶ 0.
412	```
413	Built on affine charts from the Mathlib ring-level cotangent exact
414	sequence (`KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`) and glued via the
415	compatibility above.
416	
417	The three sub-declarations `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`, `cotangentExactSeqBeta`,
418	and `cotangentExactSeq_structure` carry the remaining sorries; this theorem
419	itself is closed by assembling them.
420	
421	The composition-zero proof obligation is now an explicit existential
422	witness rather than an in-type `by sorry`, eliminating the iter-064/065
423	nested `by sorry` placeholder inside `ShortComplex.mk`. -/
424	theorem cotangent_exact_sequence (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
425	    ∃ (α : (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (relativeDifferentials g) ⟶
426	        relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g))
427	      (β : relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g) ⟶ relativeDifferentials f)
428	      (h : α ≫ β = 0),
429	      CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.Exact
430	        (CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.mk α β h) ∧
431	      CategoryTheory.Epi β := by
432	  obtain ⟨h, hex, hep⟩ := cotangentExactSeq_structure f g
433	  exact ⟨_, _, h, hex, hep⟩

435	/-! ## Smoothness and local freeness of `Ω` -/
436	
437	/-- Smoothness of a finite-presentation morphism is equivalent to `Ω_{X/S}`
438	being locally free of the given rank.
439	
440	The forward direction is the Jacobian criterion; the converse follows from
441	the cotangent exact sequence and Nakayama's lemma applied at each point. -/
442	theorem smooth_iff_locally_free_omega (f : X ⟶ S)
443	    (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f) (n : ℕ) :
444	    Smooth f ↔
445	      ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : X.Opens), x ∈ U.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
446	        let R := X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U)
447	        let M := (relativeDifferentials f).val.obj (.op U)
448	        Module.Free (↑R) (↑M) ∧ Module.rank (↑R) (↑M) = n := by
449	  sorry

451	/-! ## Cotangent space at a section -/
452	
453	/-- If `f : X ⟶ S` is smooth and `s : S ⟶ X` is a section of `f`, then the
454	cotangent space `s^* Ω_{X/S}` is a locally free `𝒪_S`-module of rank `n`.
455	In particular, if `S = Spec k` and `X` is smooth of relative dimension `n`,
456	the cotangent space at the corresponding `k`-point is an `n`-dimensional
457	`k`-vector space. -/
458	theorem cotangent_at_section (f : X ⟶ S)
459	    (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f)
460	    (s : S ⟶ X) (hs : s ≫ f = 𝟙 S) (n : ℕ)
461	    (hsmooth : Smooth f) :
462	    ∀ (x : S), ∃ (U : S.Opens), x ∈ U.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
463	      let R := S.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U)
464	      let M := ((Scheme.Modules.pullback s).obj (relativeDifferentials f)).val.obj (.op U)
465	      Module.Free (↑R) (↑M) ∧ Module.rank (↑R) (↑M) = n := by
466	  sorry

468	/-! ## Restriction of scalars to k-modules -/
469	
470	/-- The `O_C(U)`-module structure on `M(U)` viewed as a `k`-module via
471	restriction of scalars along the algebra map `k → O_C(U)`. -/
472	noncomputable abbrev moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj
473	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
474	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
475	    (M : C.left.Modules)
476	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) : ModuleCat.{u} k :=
477	  (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom).obj (M.val.obj U)
478	
479	/-- Smul-naturality lemma for `moduleKPresheafOfModules`: the additive map
480	`M.val.presheaf.map f` commutes with the `k`-action on source and target after
481	restriction of scalars. -/
482	lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_smul_compat
483	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
484	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
485	    (M : C.left.Modules)
486	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
487	    M.val.presheaf.map f ≫ (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V).smul r =
488	      (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U).smul r ≫ M.val.presheaf.map f := by
489	  -- Strategy: lift to ModuleCat.smul_naturality applied to (M.val.map f) over O_C, then transport
490	  -- the smul argument from `(kToSection C U).hom r` to `(kToSection C V).hom r` using algebraMap
491	  -- naturality.
492	  have hsmul_src : (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U).smul r =
493	      (M.val.obj U).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) := rfl
494	  have hsmul_tgt : (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V).smul r =
495	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r) := rfl
496	  rw [hsmul_src, hsmul_tgt]
497	  -- Now goal:
498	  --   M.val.presheaf.map f ≫ (M.val.obj V).smul (kV r)
499	  --     = (M.val.obj U).smul (kU r) ≫ M.val.presheaf.map f
500	  have hpres : M.val.presheaf.map f =
501	      (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat).map (M.val.map f) := by
502	    apply AddCommGrpCat.Hom.ext
503	    ext x
504	    exact PresheafOfModules.presheaf_map_apply_coe M.val f x
505	  rw [hpres]
506	  -- Now goal in terms of (forget₂).map (M.val.map f); use ModuleCat.smul_naturality:
507	  have hnat := ModuleCat.smul_naturality (M.val.map f) ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r)
508	  -- hnat has smul on the *target* of (M.val.map f) at index (kU r).
509	  -- That target is (restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (M.val.obj V), so its .smul (kU r)
510	  -- is definitionally (M.val.obj V).smul ((R.map f).hom (kU r)).
511	  -- By algebraMap_naturality, (R.map f).hom (kU r) = (kV r), which is what we need.
512	  have halg : (RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))
513	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) =
514	      (toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r := by
515	    rw [← toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_eq_kToSection, ← toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
516	    exact toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality (C := C) f r
517	  -- Convert hnat's target smul to the desired form via halg
518	  have htarget : ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars
519	        (RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))).obj (M.val.obj V)).smul
520	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) =
521	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r) := by
522	    change (M.val.obj V).smul ((RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))
523	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r)) =
524	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r)
525	    rw [halg]
526	  rw [htarget] at hnat
527	  exact hnat
528	
529	/-- The restriction map for `moduleKPresheafOfModules`, extracted as a separate
530	definition so the elaborator does not have to inline the smul-naturality proof
531	inside the main `Functor` definition. -/
532	noncomputable def moduleKPresheafOfModules_map
533	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
534	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
535	    (M : C.left.Modules)
536	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
537	    moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U ⟶ moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V :=
538	  ModuleCat.homMk (M.val.presheaf.map f) (moduleKPresheafOfModules_smul_compat C M f)
539	
540	@[simp] lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂
541	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
542	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
543	    (M : C.left.Modules)
544	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
545	    (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map
546	        (moduleKPresheafOfModules_map C M f) = M.val.presheaf.map f := by
547	  unfold moduleKPresheafOfModules_map
548	  exact ModuleCat.forget₂_map_homMk _ _
549	
550	/-- The presheaf of `k`-modules obtained from an `O_C`-module by restriction of
551	scalars along the structure morphism `k → Γ(C, U)`.
552	
553	On each open `U`, the sections are the `O_C(U)`-module `M(U)` viewed as a
554	`k`-module via the algebra map `k → O_C(U)`. The restriction maps are `k`-linear
555	by the naturality of this algebra map. -/
556	noncomputable def moduleKPresheafOfModules
557	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
558	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
559	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
560	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
561	  obj U := moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U
562	  map f := moduleKPresheafOfModules_map C M f
563	  map_id U := by
564	    apply (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map_injective
565	    rw [moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
566	    exact M.val.presheaf.map_id U
567	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
568	    apply (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map_injective
569	    rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp]
570	    simp only [moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂]
571	    exact M.val.presheaf.map_comp f g
572	
573	/-- The presheaf of `k`-modules obtained by restriction of scalars is a sheaf:
574	its underlying presheaf of abelian groups coincides with that of the original
575	`O_C`-module. -/
576	lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_isSheaf
577	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
578	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
579	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
580	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
581	      (moduleKPresheafOfModules C M) := by
582	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp _ _ (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} k) AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
583	  convert M.isSheaf using 1
584	
585	/-- The sheaf of `k`-modules obtained from an `O_C`-module by restriction of
586	scalars along the structure morphism. -/
587	noncomputable def moduleKSheafOfModules
588	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
589	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
590	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
591	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
592	  ⟨moduleKPresheafOfModules C M, moduleKPresheafOfModules_isSheaf C M⟩
593	
594	/-! ## Serre duality genus equality -/
595	
596	/-- For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over a field `k`,
597	the dimension of `H^0(C, Ω_{C/k})` equals the dimension of `H^1(C, 𝒪_C)`,
598	and both equal the genus `g(C)`.
599	
600	This is the dimension-one case of Serre duality applied to the canonical
601	sheaf `ω_C = Ω_{C/k}`. -/
602	theorem serre_duality_genus {k : Type u} [Field k]
603	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom]
604	    (hsmooth : Smooth C.hom) :
605	    Module.rank k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0) =
606	      Module.rank k
607	        (HModule k (moduleKSheafOfModules C (relativeDifferentials C.hom)) 0) := by
608	  sorry
609	
610	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

111	- **Symmetric powers / quotients of schemes by finite group actions**: absent. Gates the symmetric-power route to the Jacobian; `nonempty_jacobianWitness` is the consolidated existence claim.
112	- **`SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf`**: not in Mathlib; needed for Phase B `_structure.h_epi` stretch. Strategy: add as a project-local helper, then close `h_epi` via `PresheafOfModules.epi_iff_surjective` + `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective`.
113	
114	## Forbidden shortcuts (documented once)
115	
116	Standing rules:
117	- No new `axiom` declarations.
118	- No constant/vacuous shortcuts for protected definitions (`genus`, `Jacobian`, `ofCurve`, `LineBundle`, `PicardFunctor`, `PicardFunctor.representable`).
119	- No `≃` / `≃+` where `≃ₗ[k]` is required; no `def` where `noncomputable abbrev` is required for instance synthesis.
120	- Use `theorem` (not `instance`) for consumers taking explicit non-synthesisable arguments.
121	- Avoid `Scheme.` short-name prefix inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme` (triggers `linter.dupNamespace`).
122	- Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ _` *unconditionally* — forbidden shortcut; would force `genus C = 0` universally. **Note**: iter-073 Lane 3's refactor sets `Jacobian C := (jacobianWitness C).J`, which is NOT a shortcut — it routes through the deferred existence claim and *preserves* the mathematical content (in genus 0 the witness will have `J = 𝟙_ _`; in genus `>0` the witness's `J` is the honest Albanese object).
123	- Plan-agent must not pre-verify candidate proof bodies via `lean_run_code` / `lean_multi_attempt` (per user policy 2026-05-11); no chains of thin helper extraction across iterations.
124	
125	## Recent refactor history (compact)
126	
127	- iter-027: `Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` extracted from `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`.
128	- iter-063: `Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` (1872 LOC) split into `Cohomology/MayerVietorisCore.lean` (629 LOC), `Cohomology/MayerVietorisCover.lean` (713 LOC), `Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` (593 LOC).
129	- iter-064: `Differentials.lean` scaffolded (Phase B opened).
130	- iter-071: `JacobianWitness` bundle introduced in `Jacobian.lean` (single-witness consolidation).
131	- iter-072: `JacobianWitness` parameterised uniformly over $P$ via `isAlbaneseFor : ∀ P, IsAlbanese …`; `P` field dropped.
132	- iter-073 (this iteration, planned): `Jacobian C` dite shortcut removed — definition routes unconditionally through `jacobianWitness C`; four protected instances project unconditionally. Absorbs genus-0 rigidity into Phase C deferred existence.
133

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2024 Joël Riou. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Joël Riou
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Basic
10	
11	/-!
12	# The presheaf of differentials of a presheaf of modules
13	
14	In this file, we define the type `M.Derivation φ` of derivations
15	with values in a presheaf of `R`-modules `M` relative to
16	a morphism of `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` of presheaves of commutative rings
17	over categories `C` and `D` that are related by a functor `F : C ⥤ D`.
18	We formalize the notion of universal derivation.
19	
20	Geometrically, if `f : X ⟶ S` is a morphism of schemes (or more generally
21	a morphism of commutative ringed spaces), we would like to apply
22	these definitions in the case where `F` is the pullback functor from
23	open subsets of `S` to open subsets of `X` and `φ` is the
24	morphism $O_S ⟶ f_* O_X$.
25	
26	In order to prove that there exists a universal derivation, the target
27	of which shall be called the presheaf of relative differentials of `φ`,
28	we first study the case where `F` is the identity functor.
29	In this case where we have a morphism of presheaves of commutative
30	rings `φ' : S' ⟶ R`, we construct a derivation
31	`DifferentialsConstruction.derivation'` which is universal.
32	Then, the general case (TODO) shall be obtained by observing that
33	derivations for `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` identify to derivations
34	for `S' ⟶ R` where `S'` is the pullback by `F` of the presheaf of
35	commutative rings `S` (the data is the same: it suffices
36	to show that the two vanishing conditions `d_app` are equivalent).
37	
38	-/
39	
40	@[expose] public section
41	
42	universe v u v₁ v₂ u₁ u₂
43	
44	open CategoryTheory
45	
46	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
47	
48	namespace PresheafOfModules
49	
50	variable {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}} {F : C ⥤ D} {S' R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
51	  (M N : PresheafOfModules.{v} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
52	  (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) (φ' : S' ⟶ R)
53	
54	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`,
55	this is the type of relative `φ`-derivation of a presheaf of `R`-modules `M`. -/
56	@[ext]
57	structure Derivation where
58	  /-- the underlying additive map `R.obj X →+ M.obj X` of a derivation -/
59	  d {X : Dᵒᵖ} : R.obj X →+ M.obj X
60	  d_mul {X : Dᵒᵖ} (a b : R.obj X) : d (a * b) = a • d b + b • d a := by cat_disch
61	  d_map {X Y : Dᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X) :
62	    d (R.map f x) = M.map f (d x) := by cat_disch
63	  d_app {X : Cᵒᵖ} (a : S.obj X) : d (φ.app X a) = 0 := by cat_disch
64	
65	namespace Derivation
66	
67	-- Note: `d_app` cannot be a simp lemma because `dsimp` would
68	-- simplify the composition of functors `R ⋙ forget₂ _ _`
69	attribute [simp] d_mul d_map
70	
71	variable {M N φ}
72	
73	lemma congr_d {d d' : M.Derivation φ} (h : d = d') {X : Dᵒᵖ} (b : R.obj X) :
74	    d.d b = d'.d b := by rw [h]
75	
76	variable (d : M.Derivation φ)
77	
78	@[simp] lemma d_one (X : Dᵒᵖ) : d.d (X := X) 1 = 0 := by
79	  simpa using d.d_mul (X := X) 1 1
80	
81	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
82	/-- The postcomposition of a derivation by a morphism of presheaves of modules. -/
83	@[simps! d_apply]
84	def postcomp (f : M ⟶ N) : N.Derivation φ where
85	  d := (f.app _).hom.toAddMonoidHom.comp d.d
86	  d_map {X Y} g x := by simpa using naturality_apply f g (d.d x)
87	  d_app {X} a := by
88	    dsimp
89	    erw [d_app]
90	    rw [map_zero]
91	
92	/-- The universal property that a derivation `d : M.Derivation φ` must
93	satisfy so that the presheaf of modules `M` can be considered as the presheaf of
94	(relative) differentials of a presheaf of commutative rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`. -/
95	structure Universal where
96	  /-- An absolute derivation of `M'` descends as a morphism `M ⟶ M'`. -/
97	  desc {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
98	    (d' : M'.Derivation φ) : M ⟶ M'
99	  fac {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
100	    (d' : M'.Derivation φ) : d.postcomp (desc d') = d' := by cat_disch
101	  postcomp_injective {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
102	    (φ φ' : M ⟶ M') (h : d.postcomp φ = d.postcomp φ') : φ = φ' := by cat_disch
103	
104	attribute [simp] Universal.fac
105	
106	instance : Subsingleton d.Universal where
107	  allEq h₁ h₂ := by
108	    suffices ∀ {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
109	      (d' : M'.Derivation φ), h₁.desc d' = h₂.desc d' by
110	        cases h₁
111	        cases h₂
112	        simp only [Universal.mk.injEq]
113	        ext : 2
114	        apply this
115	    intro M' d'
116	    apply h₁.postcomp_injective
117	    simp
118	
119	end Derivation
120	
121	/-- The property that there exists a universal derivation for
122	a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`. -/
123	class HasDifferentials : Prop where
124	  exists_universal_derivation : ∃ (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
125	      (d : M.Derivation φ), Nonempty d.Universal
126	
127	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings `φ : S ⟶ R`,
128	this is the type of relative `φ`-derivation of a presheaf of `R`-modules `M`. -/
129	abbrev Derivation' : Type _ := M.Derivation (F := 𝟭 D) φ'
130	
131	namespace Derivation'
132	
133	variable {M φ'}
134	
135	@[simp]
136	lemma d_app (d : M.Derivation' φ') {X : Dᵒᵖ} (a : S'.obj X) :
137	    d.d (φ'.app X a) = 0 :=
138	  Derivation.d_app d _
139	
140	/-- The derivation relative to the morphism of commutative rings `φ'.app X` induced by
141	a derivation relative to a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings. -/
142	noncomputable def app (d : M.Derivation' φ') (X : Dᵒᵖ) : (M.obj X).Derivation (φ'.app X) :=
143	  ModuleCat.Derivation.mk (fun b ↦ d.d b)
144	
145	@[simp]
146	lemma app_apply (d : M.Derivation' φ') {X : Dᵒᵖ} (b : R.obj X) :
147	    (d.app X).d b = d.d b := rfl
148	
149	section
150	
151	variable (d : ∀ (X : Dᵒᵖ), (M.obj X).Derivation (φ'.app X))
152	
153	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
154	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings `φ'`, this is the
155	in derivation `M.Derivation' φ'` that is given by a compatible family of derivations
156	with values in the modules `M.obj X` for all `X`. -/
157	def mk (d_map : ∀ ⦃X Y : Dᵒᵖ⦄ (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X),
158	    (d Y).d ((R.map f) x) = (M.map f) ((d X).d x)) : M.Derivation' φ' where
159	  d {X} := AddMonoidHom.mk' (d X).d (by simp)
160	
161	variable (d_map : ∀ ⦃X Y : Dᵒᵖ⦄ (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X),
162	      (d Y).d ((R.map f) x) = (M.map f) ((d X).d x))
163	
164	@[simp]
165	lemma mk_app (X : Dᵒᵖ) : (mk d d_map).app X = d X := rfl
166	
167	/-- Constructor for `Derivation.Universal` in the case `F` is the identity functor. -/
168	def Universal.mk {d : M.Derivation' φ'}
169	    (desc : ∀ {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)}
170	      (_ : M'.Derivation' φ'), M ⟶ M')
171	    (fac : ∀ {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)}
172	      (d' : M'.Derivation' φ'), d.postcomp (desc d') = d')
173	    (postcomp_injective : ∀ {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)}
174	      (α β : M ⟶ M'), d.postcomp α = d.postcomp β → α = β) : d.Universal where
175	  desc := desc
176	  fac := fac
177	  postcomp_injective := postcomp_injective
178	
179	end
180	
181	end Derivation'
182	
183	namespace DifferentialsConstruction
184	
185	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
186	/-- The presheaf of relative differentials of a morphism of presheaves of
187	commutative rings. -/
188	@[simps -isSimp]
189	noncomputable def relativeDifferentials' :
190	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _) where
191	  obj X := CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app X)
192	  -- Have to hint `g' := R.map f` below, or it gets unfolded weirdly.
193	  map f := CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.map (g' := R.map f) (φ'.naturality f)
194	  -- Without `dsimp`, `ext` doesn't pick up the right lemmas.
195	  map_id _ := by dsimp; ext; simp
196	  map_comp _ _ := by dsimp; ext; simp
197	
198	attribute [simp] relativeDifferentials'_obj
199	
200	@[simp]
201	lemma relativeDifferentials'_map_d {X Y : Dᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X) :
202	    DFunLike.coe (α := CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app X))
203	      (β := fun _ ↦ CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app Y))
204	      (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (R := ↑(R.obj X)) ((relativeDifferentials' φ').map f))

61	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules is fully faithful. -/
62	def fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules : (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).FullyFaithful :=
63	  SheafOfModules.fullyFaithfulForget _

72	noncomputable def toPresheaf : X.Modules ⥤ TopCat.Presheaf Ab X :=
73	  toPresheafOfModules X ⋙ PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _

84	section Presheaf
85	
86	/-- The underlying abelian presheaf of an `𝒪ₓ`-module. -/
87	noncomputable def presheaf (M : X.Modules) : TopCat.Presheaf Ab X := M.1.presheaf
88

90	scoped[AlgebraicGeometry] notation3 "Γ(" M ", " U ")" => (Scheme.Modules.presheaf M).obj (.op U)
91	
92	instance : Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, U) := (M.val.obj (.op U)).isModule

94	variable (M) in
95	@[simp] lemma map_smul (i : U ⟶ V) (r : Γ(X, V)) (x : Γ(M, V)) :
96	    M.presheaf.map i.op (r • x) = X.presheaf.map i.op r • M.presheaf.map i.op x :=
97	  M.val.map_smul _ _ _
98	
99	/-- The underlying map between abelian presheaves of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
100	noncomputable def Hom.mapPresheaf (φ : M ⟶ N) : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf :=
101	  (toPresheaf X).map φ
102	
103	/-- The application of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules to sections. -/
104	def Hom.app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : X.Opens) : Γ(M, U) ⟶ Γ(N, U) :=
105	  (forget₂ _ _).map (φ.val.app (.op U))
106	
107	@[simp] lemma mapPresheaf_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U) : φ.mapPresheaf.app U = φ.app U.unop := rfl
108	
109	@[simp]
110	lemma Hom.app_smul (φ : M ⟶ N) (r : Γ(X, U)) (x : Γ(M, U)) :
111	    φ.app U (r • x) = r • φ.app U x :=
112	  (φ.val.app (.op U)).hom.map_smul r x
113	
114	@[simp] lemma Hom.add_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ + ψ).app U = φ.app U + ψ.app U := rfl
115	@[simp] lemma Hom.sub_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ - ψ).app U = φ.app U - ψ.app U := rfl
116	@[simp] lemma Hom.zero_app : (0 : M ⟶ N).app U = 0 := rfl
117	@[simp] lemma Hom.id_app (M : X.Modules) : (𝟙 M :).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
118	@[simp] lemma Hom.comp_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (ψ : N ⟶ K) : (φ ≫ ψ).app U = φ.app U ≫ ψ.app U := rfl
119	
120	@[ext]
121	lemma hom_ext (f g : M ⟶ N) (H : ∀ U, f.app U = g.app U) : f = g := by
122	  apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
123	  ext U x
124	  exact congr($(H U.unop) x)

126	lemma isSheaf (M : X.Modules) : M.presheaf.IsSheaf := SheafOfModules.isSheaf M
127	
128	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_obj : (toPresheaf X).obj M = M.presheaf := rfl
129	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_map : (toPresheaf X).map φ = φ.mapPresheaf := rfl
130	
131	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
132	lemma Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app {M N : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} :
133	    IsIso φ ↔ ∀ U, IsIso (φ.app U) := by
134	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (toPresheaf X), NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
135	  simp [Opposite.op_surjective.forall]
136

138	
139	@[simp, push ←]
140	lemma inv_app [IsIso φ] : (inv φ).app U = inv (φ.app U) := by
141	  apply IsIso.eq_inv_of_hom_inv_id
142	  simp [← Hom.comp_app]
